stem: A rectangular garden measures 20 feet by 12 feet. A circular fountain with a diameter of 8 feet is located in the center of the garden. What is the area, in square feet, of the garden NOT covered by the fountain?
explanation: Rectangle area: $20 \times 12 = 240$ square feet. The fountain has diameter 8, so radius $r = 4$. Circle area: $\pi(4)^2 = 16\pi$. Remaining area: $240 - 16\pi$ square feet. The answer is A.

distractorRationale.B: Uses the diameter as the radius: $\pi(8)^2 = 64\pi$, giving $240 - 64\pi$.
distractorRationale.C: Uses the circumference formula instead of area: $2\pi(4) = 8\pi$, giving $240 - 8\pi$.
distractorRationale.D: Computes the perimeter of the rectangle instead of the area: $2(20 + 12) = 64$, giving $64 - 16\pi$.
